rulings.Case Title: KHP III SUTTER LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. ASSESMENT APPEALS BOARD FOR THE CITY AND COUNTY OF
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 15, 2018 Line 12, PETITIONER KHP III SUTTER LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY's Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus Petitioner KHP III SF Sutter LLC's petition for writ of administrative mandamus is granted. "[W]here a government agent's actions have induced noncompliance with procedural requirements, the government could be estopped from asserting those procedural bars against a citizen's recovery." (J.H. McKnight Ranch, Inc. v. Franchise Tax Bd. (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 978, 979.) Respondent Assessment Appeals Board affirmatively advised KHP by the language in its "Information and Instructions for Assessment Appeal Application" that KHP could file an appeal to the supplemental assessment within 60 days of the date of the tax bill. The Board is therefore equitably estopped from rejecting KHP's appeal of the supplemental assessment. Contrary to the Board's assertions, estoppel applies because the equitable remedy is necessary to avoid a "grave injustice" to KHP in losing its chance to contest the accuracy of the $44,600,000 tax bill assessed against it. (Steinhart v. County of Los Angeles (2010) 47 Cal.4th 1298, 1315.) "A taxpayer has a right to a hearing on his property tax assessment, and if an application for a hearing is denied for insufficient legal reason there is a denial of due process." (Midstate Theatres, Inc. v. Board of Supervisors (1975) 46 Cal.App.3d 204, 208.) The Board's determination that KHP's reliance on the language in the Instructions was unreasonable constitutes prejudicial abuse of discretion because it is not supported by substantial evidence in the administrative record. (CCP 1094.5(b), (c).) Nothing in the notice of supplemental assessment mailed to KHP eliminates or remediates the harmful consequences of the now repudiated language contained in the Instructions that was relied on by KHP. That the Board subsequently obtained approval from the State Board of Equalization to correct the language relied on by KHP is tantamount to a confession of error by the Board and compels the conclusion that KHP not be denied the critical right to appeal the supplemental assessment because it relied on erroneous information disseminated by the Board. KHP is entitled to a writ of mandate requiring the Board to accept KHP's administrative appeal as timely and to determine the merits of that appeal. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for KHP is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed judgment in conformity with the tentative ruling and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. THAI MING CHIU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Set Aside Order Appointing Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 15, 2018 Line 3, DEFENDANT DEMAS YAN's MOTION To Set Aside Order Appointing Receiver Defendant Demas Yan's motion to set aside order appointing receiver is denied. There are three separate and independent reasons requiring denial of this motion. First, Mr. Yan is in default status now and was at the time the motion for appointment of receiver was filed, so he had no right to contest the appointment of a receiver. Second, Mr. Yan's counsel of record, Mr. Boasberg, was served with all relevant papers on a timely basis. Third, Mr. Yan has failed to provide any grounds for opposing the appointment of a receiver. rulings.Case Title: LAURA ALBERT ET AL VS. INTERNATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 15, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS LAURA ALBERT, TREVOR KNOOP'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS Judge Van Aken will hear this motion in department 506 at 10:30 a.m. Grant the motion to quash in part and deny it in part. The standard for obtaining access to Plaintiff's medical and educational records is direct relevance. That standard is met as to all categories requested by the subpoena except "student health records." As to that category, the parties appear to agree Defendants already have medical records and Defendants make no argument that there are unique health records maintained by SFUSD that are relevant to Plaintiff's claims. The motion to quash is granted as to student health records. As to other categories of documents, Plaintiff has put at issue the effects of his alleged injury on his academic performance. The motion to quash is denied as to those records, which shall be produced pursuant to the protective order in this case. The parties' requests for sanctions are denied because no party's position lacked substantial justification. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: THEDA FUNG VS. SUSANA MANCIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion: 1. For Order Compelling Further Responses To Petitioner Served Discovery, 2. For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Compel Further Responses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 15, 2018, Line 4 PLAINTIFF THEDA FUNG'S Motion: 1. For Order Compelling Further Responses To Petitioner Served Discovery, 2. For Sanctions Judge Van Aken will hear this motion in department 506 at 10:30 a.m. On the motion to compel Defendant Susana Mancia to serve further responses to Form Interrogatories Set No. 1, the Court orders further responses to FI 15.1. Defendant Mancia can properly be required to identify what facts alleged in Plaintiff's complaint she believes are untrue and what facts, witnesses, and documents she relies on for her belief. With regard to FI 12.1, this interrogatory is not tailored to a complaint concerning a series of incidents and the motion to compel further responses is denied. On the motion to compel Defendant Mancia to serve further responses to Special Interrogatories Set No. 1, the Court grants the motion and orders further responses as to SI 1, SI 2, SI 6, SI 7, and SI 8. The Court denies the motion as to SI 5 and SI 9 because Defendant has responded and Plaintiff's objections are simply that she disagrees with the responses. On the motion to compel Defendant Mancia to serve further responses to Plaintiff's Demand for Production of Documents Set No. 1, the Court grants the motion. Defendant's opposition papers contend that all responsive documents have been produced but that is not reflected in the response to the demand for production or in the declarations that Defendant filed in opposition to the discovery motion. On the motion to compel Defendant MGM to serve further responses to Plaintiff's Demand for Production of Documents Set No. 1, Defendants' opposition offers no argument and the Court grants the motion. The Court denies the parties' respective requests for sanctions. Plaintiff's request is denied because Plaintiff did not submit a supporting declaration showing the time and expense of seeking a discovery order. Defendant's request is denied because Plaintiff's motion does not lack substantial justification. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: LILLIAN FORMAN ET AL VS. SUTTER HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Compliance With Deposition Subpoena Of Patientpop, Inc. For Business Records And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 15, 2018 Line 7, PLAINTIFFs LILLIAN FORMAN, JULIE FORMAN, CHANCE FORMAN, and KRISTA THRASHER's Motion For Order Compelling Compliance With Deposition Subpoena Of Patientpop, Inc. For Business Records And Request For Monetary Sanctions Plaintiffs' motion for an order compelling compliance with their deposition subpoena to non-party PatientPop, Inc. is granted in part. No later than October 31, 2018 PatientPop is required to produce all documents in its possession, custody and control regarding the reputation and competence of defendant Rachel Shu including all reviews of Dr. Shu by persons for whom she has provided any medical services without regard to when the documents were created or when Dr. Shu performed services. PatientPop is required to redact the names and contact or identifying information of any patients of Dr. Shu prior to producing the documents. Documents regarding the reputation and competence of Dr. Shu meet the standard of discovery relevance and there has been no showing that producing such documents is unduly burdensome. All other documents sought by the subpoena do not meet the standard of discovery relevance. Because the positions asserted by both plaintiffs and PatientPop were substantially justified, all requests for monetary sanctions are denied. rulings.Case Title: VATCHE CABAYAN M.D ET AL VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 15, 2018 Line 13, DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSANTION INSURANCE FUND's, A PUBLIC MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant State Compensation Insurance Fund's anti-SLAPP motion to strike the first and second causes of action for intentional interference with prospective economic relations and breach of the implied covenant of good fair and fair dealing in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiffs Vatche Cabayan and V. Cabayan Med. Corp. is denied as to both causes of action. Plaintiffs' allegations concerning State Fund's disparaging statements are so factually deficient that they preclude a determination whether they arise from State Fund's exercise of activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute. As explained in Central Valley Hospitalists v. Dignity Health (2018) 19 Cal.App.5th 203, the proper course is to sustain a demurrer to plaintiffs' factually deficient claims and deny this anti-SLAPP motion on the grounds that State Fund is unable to satisfy its first prong burden. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Counsel for State Fund is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN HAUCK VS. ALEXANDRA INVESTMENTS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Transferring Case To Appropriate Court (Change Of Venue) [Ccp 396b]; Declaration Of Emily A. Mahoney; Declaration Of Louy Suliman
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 15, 2018 Line 9, DEFENDANT ANGELO'S TOWING Motion For Order Transferring Case To Appropriate Court (Change Of Venue) [Ccp 396b] Defendant Angelo's Towing's motion to transfer case is denied. Venue is proper per CCP 395(a) because this action was filed in the county where one of the defendants resides. The complaint does not show that defendant Robert Morrison, a San Francisco resident, was improperly joined or joined in bad faith. Joinder statutes are liberally construed and where a plaintiff is uncertain as to the extent each tortfeasor is responsible for his injuries, joinder of the all alleged tortfeasors per CCP 379(c) is proper. (Kraft v. Smith (1944) 24 Cal.2d 124, 128-129). The complaint states a cause of action against the San Francisco resident co-defendant thus evidencing good faith in his joinder to the action. (Gutierrez v. Superior Court, City and County of San Francisco (1966) 243 Cal.App.2d 710, 725-726, ("the usual test by which to determine whether a defendant has been joined in good faith is whether or not the complaint states a cause of action against him.")). There are no grounds to award attorney fees to any of the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Hauck is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: REGIONAL PROTECTION AGENCY, INC. VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 128.7
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 15, 2018 Line 10, DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'s Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 128.7 Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'s motion for sanctions pursuant to CCP 128.7 is granted in small part. Plaintiff Regional Protection Agency, Inc.'s first, eighth and ninth causes of action for breach of contract, quantum meruit and promissory estoppel are stricken. All other monetary and non-monetary sanctions sought by Uber are denied. RPA's request for monetary sanctions is also denied. This motion should never have been brought and is arguably an abuse of the 128.7 procedure notwithstanding that Uber is correct that RPA should have dismissed its claims seeking payment for services once RPA acknowledged that it had received full payment for services. The portions of Uber's motion seeking more than $100,000 in sanctions and the striking of the second through seventh causes of action has a strong flavor of a bullying tactic by a large company with huge resources against a litigation adversary with substantially less resources, wholly apart from the procedural and substantive lack of merit to those portions of the motion. Uber's failure to serve RPA with the declaration of Michael Zito at least 21 days before filing this motion precludes Uber from obtaining any monetary sanctions. Uber's request for an order striking the second through seventh causes of action grounds (i.e., its payment of all monies due for services and its failure to receive the employment contracts) does not take into account that those claims allege misconduct by Uber other than failure to pay monies for services and interference with contract (such as misuse of proprietary information) and that some or all of those claims can succeed even if there were no written contracts and even if (contrary to RPA's allegation) the contracts were at will contracts. Because RPA should have dismissed the first, eighth and ninth causes of action after being served with 21 day notice of Uber's intent to file this motion and its counsel should have been more candid about the nature of the employment contracts, RPA's request for monetary sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for RPA is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
